title: This AI Chinese model is 15X cheaper?
summary: MiniMax M3 is a long-context AI model that uses Sparse Attention to process documents 15.6x faster than comparable models while reducing compute costs to one-twentieth of the previous generation. It achieves efficiency by selectively focusing on relevant parts of input rather than processing everything.
writeup: The video examines MiniMax M3, a long-context model that applies Sparse Attention to handle documents 15.6x faster than comparable models. The central claim is efficiency: compute costs drop to one-twentieth of the previous generation by selectively attending to relevant parts of the input rather than processing everything. The takeaway for long-context and local inference work is that selective attention, not brute-force processing, drives both the speed and cost gains.
